dialogueChunks.intro: Sit down, {player_name}. What I'm about to tell you will make your blood boil, and I need you thinking clearly.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Three nights ago, a band of {enemy_type} hit the storehouse in {location} and killed the night watchman: an old man who never hurt anyone in his life. They took every last crate of {quest_item} and left him bleeding in the doorway. {target_npc} found the body at dawn. We tracked the raiders {location_hint}, but the guards here won't pursue beyond the settlement boundary. Someone needs to bring those monsters to justice.
dialogueChunks.outro: That old man deserved better than dying alone on a cold floor. Will you make this right?

playerResponses.text: They murdered an innocent man. I'll hunt them down.
Justice and vengeance both pay the same. I'm in.
Vengeance is a dangerous road. I'll pass.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I'm going to be straight with you. I'm not asking for charity. I'm asking for justice.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Someone poisoned the well in {location}. Not an accident: deliberate. Three people are dead, including {target_npc}'s daughter. The alchemist found traces of a compound that only grows in the caves {location_hint}, and the tracks leading away from the well belong to {enemy_type}. They've been raiding our trade routes for months, but this? This is something else entirely. This is war.
dialogueChunks.outro: Poisoning a well is the lowest act imaginable. Whoever ordered it needs to answer for what they've done.

playerResponses.text: Poisoning a water supply is an act of war. I'll find whoever is responsible.
Tracking poisoners through caves sounds dangerous. What's the bounty?
This sounds like a feud that goes deeper than I want to get involved in.

dialogueChunks.intro: You look like someone who understands that some wrongs can't be forgiven, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A merchant caravan was slaughtered on the road {location_hint} last week. Not robbed: slaughtered. The goods were left scattered in the mud, untouched. {target_npc} was the only survivor, hiding under a wagon with a broken leg. They saw everything. The {enemy_type} did it for sport, laughing while good people screamed. The guards filed a report. A report. As if paperwork brings back the dead.
dialogueChunks.outro: Those merchants had families waiting for them to come home. Someone needs to deliver the answer their families deserve.

playerResponses.text: Killing for sport? They won't be laughing when I find them.
I'll track them, but I need supplies and information first.
I hunt monsters, not ghosts. The trail is probably cold by now.

dialogueChunks.intro: Buy you a drink, {player_name}? Good. You'll need it after you hear what I have to say.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The blacksmith's apprentice was beaten half to death behind the tavern last night by {enemy_type} who've been shaking down traders in {location} for weeks. The boy refused to hand over the {quest_item} he'd been commissioned to deliver, so they made an example of him. {target_npc} pulled him out of the gutter at sunrise. The guards know who's responsible: a crew holed up in a camp {location_hint}. But knowing and acting are two different things around here.
dialogueChunks.outro: That boy is sixteen years old and may never use his right hand again. If the guards won't act, someone else has to.

playerResponses.text: Show me where this crew is hiding. They'll answer for what they did.
Sixteen years old? I'll make them regret ever setting foot in {location}.
Getting involved in local gang disputes isn't my fight.
